name: La Tomatina
traditions: Massive tomato fight in town streets; ham on a greased pole contest; water cannons to start the fight; parade of trucks with tomatoes.
historicalBackground: La Tomatina began spontaneously in 1945 when young people started a food fight during a parade in Buñol. Banned and revived, it became official in 1957.
description: The world's largest food fight, where thousands hurl over 100 tons of ripe tomatoes at each other in the streets of Buñol, Spain.
